label: Formal verification of models

node.label: Formal verification of models
node.definition: Formal model verification proves that a model satisfies a specified property over an input region or abstraction, such as robustness, bounded output, or monotonicity.
node.why_it_matters: It can replace sampled evidence with mathematical guarantees for narrowly defined high-consequence properties.
limitations: Verification scales poorly with model size and nonlinear complexity, and the specified property may omit real hazards.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Formal verification of models has an `evaluates_or_constrains` dependency on Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations because Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations is modeled as evaluation, constraint, audit, or governance support.

In this atlas graph, Formal verification of models has a `verifies_with` dependency on Constraint satisfaction, SAT & SMT solving because Constraint satisfaction, SAT & SMT solving is modeled as evidence or constraints for verification.

In this atlas graph, Formal verification of models has an `evaluates_or_constrains` dependency on Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation because Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation is modeled as evaluation, constraint, audit, or governance support.

In this atlas graph, Formal verification of models has an `evaluates_or_constrains` dependency on Ablation, interventions & causal analysis because Ablation, interventions & causal analysis is modeled as evaluation, constraint, audit, or governance support.
